title: RFC: manifest.list.Add: default to assuming variant=v8 for arm64 images
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

When adding images with Architecture="arm64" to a manifest list, default to adding Variant="v8" to the description of the image's target platform, since it can be overwritten later, and it's the only valid value listed in the OCI image index spec.

#### How to verify it

This shouldn't impact testing.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

Is this the correct thing?  If so, should we set a variant by default for 32-bit ARM ("arm") as well?

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```
Adding an arm64 image to a manifest list will default to marking it for "Variant" = "v8".
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *nalind* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Is this fixing anything specific? https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/master/image-index.md lists it as optional.

OTOH c/image/internal/pkg/platform ’s `baseVariants` clearly shows that images with variant="v8" do show up in the wild.

IIRC looking up history of that value will point at conversations that discuss the presence/absence/desirability of a “base variant” for ARM32 as well.

---

Stylistically, hiding this bit of mostly—policy fairly deep in a long implementation piece like this feels a bit off. OTOH c/image is currently hiding the data because there wasn’t an external user and we didn’t want to commit to a stable API without one, so…
I don't think anything's broken at this point - both dockerd and containers/image will fall back to accepting an entry with Variant="" when looking for a 64-bit image on an aarch64 system.

> @nalind Is this still held?

The feedback so far is that we don't need or want to do what this patch does, and absent any other feedback, I'd lean toward closing it without merging.
